ops.title: cc-interact (sibling repo) release mechanics — npm-publish-only release.yml, tags without GitHub Releases, changelog historically behind tags
ops.body:
The sibling repo `/Users/yasyf/Code/cc-interact` releases like this: `release.yml` triggers on `v*` tags and ONLY npm-publishes `@cc-interact/react` from `web/` (skip-if-already-published); it cuts no GitHub Release object (only v0.1.0 ever had one). Go consumers just need the pushed tag. Its CI (`ci.yml`) runs go vet + go test -race + web build — no golangci-lint, no govulncheck. As of 2026-07-10 the daemon auth/bind feature shipped as **v0.6.0** (commit 25507a5); v0.5.0 was the web ToastStack release. The CHANGELOG had been ~4 tags behind until the v0.6.0 landing reconciled it.

**Why:** Two landing attempts stalled on stale assumptions about this repo's tag/release shape (a planned "v0.5.0" tag turned out already cut and npm-published mid-flight).

**How to apply:** Before tagging cc-interact, `git ls-remote --tags origin` first; bump semver from the latest *remote* tag, not from a commit-message hint. Expect no GitHub Release; watching release.yml means watching the npm skip/publish step. Related: [[cc-present-ios-client]].
